title: calea repanda
intro: Calea repanda, also known as Mexican Damiana or Old Woman's Broom, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Calea repanda include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic and digestive aid. Calea repanda can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
